evidence.text: Job stability, recession proof, good working environment, excellent insurance and benefits Good stable company
Benefits, Benefits, Benefits, Perks, and Benefits Salary
The compensation is decent. Great benefits: 26 weeks of maternity leave and 21 days of PTO Fair compensation and great benefits
good company benefits, health insurance Good company benefits

Benefits are unlike anything I've ever had before. It's especially great if you have a family or looking to have a family in the future. It's also generally a pretty stable company to work for. Amazing benefits, good company, but inflexible
Great work life balance. Your day ends at 5 if youâd like it to. Amazing people. Great work life balance and people
Stable pay, great work life balance Great work life balance
Amazing work life balance Talented co-workers Good DevX Team Flat Hierarchy Good Work Life Balance & Decent Projects
Good work life balance Nice easy going people Good work life balance

- Work life balance is great - Great culture with learning; encouraged to fail and grow - Interesting projects in the financial tech industry - Lots of internal mobility Good place for growth and work life balance
The offices are widely-heralded as cool places to work, from fish tanks to snacks. As leadership will tell you, they print money every year. Poor pay, confusing environment, bad leadership

Strong brand and reputation in the industry â¢ Competitive benefits and compensation, especially for high performers â¢ Access to excellent resources, data, and technology that help in sales â¢ Opportunity to work with smart and driven colleagues â¢ If you have a supportive manager, career growth can be strong Account manager
Great benefits, dynamic company, nice office Good benefits, little opportunities for growth

Strong engineering culture with emphasis on code quality, system design, and reliability. Exposure to large-scale, real-time data systems used by global financial institutions. Smart, collaborative colleagues who are open to knowledge sharing and mentorship. High-impact work with a steep learning curve
Tremendous benefits - inclusive of health and education Great colleagues Opportunities to grow Great company, but tough leadership team

- Internal Mobility: Switching teams within the company is straightforward and encouraged. - Sponsored Participation in External Leagues: Opportunities to join and compete in sports leagues like basketball, tennis, and ping pong, with company support. - BOB Events (Best of Bloomberg): Access to incredible charity activities and exclusive events, such as complimentary tickets to the - Apple Circus with perks like an...
collaborative environment, flexible working hours Great collaborative environment
Good work environment Good work environment Good
Little stress and kind people Kind company with little pip, but refresh warning
Great collaborative environment. Always something going on so it keeps you busy which makes your day go by faster. Excellent support from management (at least in my area) Many opportunities
Best coworkers ever. Some of the best people I have met, as well as the network you can build through the role and your clients its great for the exposure Toxic place to work at Management doest care
Pantry Benefits 40 hour work schedule Toxic Workplace

Great job security, benefits and opportunities to do work across print, radio, digital and TV. For ambitious people, you can land book deals, get shows and so on. A lot of autonomy. Great Opportunities, But High Pressure & Expectations

You have to be on 24/7/365. Still a lot of old-school editors there who like to kcik and scream, though that's gone down considerably in the past 10 years or so. Great place to work but stressful

At Bloomberg, the seniority ceiling is a tangible barrier â senior roles are often held for decades, creating an operational bottleneck that restricts advancement. Even leading a team or delivering outsized results does not guarantee upward mobility, leaving high-performing employees overextended and carrying increasing workloads without recognition or a clear path forward. Do yourself a favor â prioritize your...
not clear career growth and promotion Data Scientist

Poor management practices Bad managers are not held accountable Weak minded men Woke company culture No remote work Poor mentorship No career growth Proprietary tech - makes you less competitive in the job market. Focus on widely used tech (Python, React, SQL, Kafka, NoSQL, etc) Good to have on resume
no upward mobility and you will not have marketable skills Kinda a dead end
Skills are not transferable. No visibility of how comp works. No visibility in comp raise
Management doesn't always recognize high performers Great work/life balance, friendly people
salary, upper management, not challenging work, lots of red tape, complacency not challenging work, hard to innovate but great work life balance
Things move a bit slow. Great work life balance
Not wfh flexibility just 1 day a week Great company and great benefits

$75 Stable Employment But Lack of Accountability

Nasty managers. Retaliation and gossip Micromanaging and backstabbing
Corporate politics is the way to get things done and advance. Horizontal structure doesn't always allow for upward growth. Cut throat attitudes. Great company but some corporate politics
Very toxic culture. your voice isnât heard unless youâre at the very top of the food chain. Very minimal career progression and learning offered. Toxic culture
1. Sometimes bureaucratic. 2. Cannot fully remote. Company with good WLB, colleagues

As Mike steps back the bureaucracy is gradually eating away at everything that made this company special. I used to feel valued and empowered to make a difference - in the end, I was just a cog. I get the RTO policy, and thatâs 100% the companyâs right, but it would have been nice if someone in HR or management actually gave a fig when I had to give up a job and team I loved to take care of my family. No âthan...
Not much. Typical corporate red tape. Great company
I do not have any cons great work environment
Management is poorly trained and lack leadership skills. Toxic Environment
